characters.physical_details: Short, silvering, broad-palmed, with one clouded eye.
characters.occupation_status: Village midwife and private birth-record keeper; relocated under appeal.
characters.literacy_languages: Basic Bangla reading and name/date writing; speaks char dialect.

state_transitions.change: Search exposes ledger rumor; authority damaged.
Learns Sufia copied eastern pages; chooses full-ledger route.
Reads ledger publicly; own household sent to appeal.
Gives ledger to Aminul; moves to Shed 3, Bay 2.

characters.availability: Alive; ends in Nabinpara temporary accommodation.

characters.physical_details: Lean, quick, with careful hands and a wrist burn.

key_relationships.aminul_haque: Official route to training or exclusion.
key_relationships.mofiz_uddin: Broker turning her file into scandal.
key_relationships.sufia_begum: Neighbor tied to the ledger mess.

characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes Bangla well; handles forms and phone entries.

state_transitions.change: Clinic-training plan becomes explicit.
Lip split and hip bruised in search.
Learns Saleha died after her birth and Halima used Monir Hossain on her papers.
Finds Monir's trail broken by conflicting father names.
Household marked disputed pending appeal.
Chooses Halima's household by her own mouth.

characters.availability: Alive; off-char for the chapter_12 day trip; ends in Shed 3, Bay 2.

characters.physical_details: Trim moustache, pressed shirts, files kept away from sweat.

key_relationships.halima_khatun: Witness he needs but cannot fully protect.

characters.occupation_status: Upazila records officer handling Kadam Char batch.
characters.literacy_languages: Formal Bangla literacy; works with bureaucratic forms.

state_transitions.change: Secures household-affidavit route.
Publicly backs revised house list.
Takes ledger into appeal custody.

characters.availability: Alive; present through verification, hearing, and transfer.

characters.physical_details: Broad-shouldered, sun-darkened, with a chipped tooth and rope-burned palms.

key_relationships.halima_khatun: Midwife who wrote his paper line.

characters.occupation_status: Fisherman and laborer with a disputed western-edge claim.
characters.literacy_languages: Signs his name and reads names and numbers slowly.

state_transitions.change: Suspects father line is false.
Learns Rahima is his mother; file stays on appeal.
Calls Rahima Ma before she dies.
Moves on death hold to Transit Shed 4, Bay 7 after Rahima dies before countersignature.

characters.availability: Alive; ends in transit pending appeal.

characters.physical_details: Small, underfed, smoke-dark shawl, deeply cracked heels.

key_relationships.kashem_sheikh: Biological son kept hidden in public terms.

characters.occupation_status: Widow selling puffed rice, keeping goats, and living on credit and day labor.
characters.literacy_languages: Not formally literate; recognizes some names and numbers by shape.

state_transitions.change: First presses Kashem's maternity claim aloud.
Publicly names Kashem as her son and witnesses his file.
Dies after Kashem acknowledges her.

characters.physical_details: Fine-boned and carefully controlled in dress and manner.

key_relationships.halima_khatun: Elder who moved her into the aunt-line.
key_relationships.mofiz_uddin: Broker trying to weaponize her paper history.

characters.occupation_status: Dried-fish, egg, and poultry seller under a verified aunt-line claim.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads household papers, writes simple Bangla, speaks local dialect.

state_transitions.change: Keeps aunt-line claim despite cost to birth mother.
Accepted under household affidavit.
Marriage turns guarded after public exposure.
Moves to C-12 and offers Halima/Nura floor space.

characters.physical_details: Tall, slightly stooped, with grease under his nails.

key_relationships.halima_khatun: Old rescuer whose history now burdens his house.

characters.occupation_status: Boat mechanic and ferry hand with engine-parts debt.
characters.literacy_languages: Signs his name and reads work numbers and short entries.

state_transitions.change: Backs aunt-line claim as safest route.
Defends current household with debt papers.
Keeps the house intact but controls the papers more tightly.

characters.physical_details: Heavyset, well-dressed, with dyed beard edges.
characters.occupation_status: Political broker, fertilizer dealer, and queue gatekeeper.
characters.literacy_languages: Literate in Bangla and skilled at patronage paperwork.

state_transitions.change: Engineers search and ledger rumor.
Copied-page scheme exposed.
Fails to stop the house list, though Halima's file stays on appeal.

characters.physical_details: Broad-faced, slightly limping, with betel-stained lips and quick hands.

key_relationships.halima_khatun: Former assistant who betrays her, then confesses.
key_relationships.nura_begum: Younger neighbor caught in the same paper web.
key_relationships.mofiz_uddin: Man she wrongly trusted with copied pages.

characters.occupation_status: Tea-and-betel stall owner near the ghat; former birth assistant.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads simple Bangla, writes names imperfectly, speaks local dialect.

state_transitions.change: Confesses copying eastern-side ledger pages for Mofiz.

characters.availability: Alive; ends in Nabinpara two rows over from Halima and Nura.

timeline.story_start: Late monsoon in Bangladesh, when a launch announces Kadam Char's evacuation and verification scheme.
timeline.estimated_span: Five weeks from notice to Nabinpara arrival.

seasonal_track.season: Late monsoon: wet heat, fragile banks, and document-damaging damp.
Monsoon easing: drier paths, office travel, and tightening deadlines.
Early dry-season onset: harder ground, bank collapse, dismantling, and relocation.

key_events.event: Launch order ties compensation to verified households.
First school verification exposes broken paper chains.
Nura's clinic plan collides with her blocked father line.
Rahima first voices Kashem's maternity claim.
Friday-noon deadline and two-witness rule are posted.
Search injures Nura and exposes Halima's house.
Halima tells Nura the Saleha/Monir truth; Monir's papers prove unusable.
Mosque assembly hardens house-list and blood-list camps.
Rehearsals trim households until Sufia's confession changes the plan.
Magistrate accepts most household affidavits; Kashem and Halima/Nura go to appeal.
Rahima dies after Kashem calls her Ma; backlash follows.
Eastern bank collapse forces final loading; Halima gives Aminul the ledger.
Nabinpara separates numbered houses from transit sheds.

geography.primary_setting: Kadam Char, a fictional Jamuna silt island under Gaibandha district administration, later paired with the inland Nabinpara Relocation Colony.

key_locations.name: Halima's house
key_locations.details: Low tin-roof house near mid-char, with birth supplies and the hidden ledger on a loft shelf.

key_locations.name: Kadam Char Primary School
key_locations.details: Verification camp, rehearsal room, hearing site, and list-posting wall.

key_locations.name: Mosque courtyard
key_locations.details: Public debate site where house-list and blood-list camps form.

key_locations.name: Western-edge huts
key_locations.details: Poorer, erosion-hit cluster including Rahima's hut and many weak paper claims.

key_locations.name: Eastern high ground
key_locations.details: More stable, politically valuable ground where Mofiz's copied pages matter.

key_locations.name: Ghat and Sufia's tea stall
key_locations.details: Boat landing, rumor exchange, witness market, and final loading bottleneck.

key_locations.name: Upazila offices and bus-stand lodge
key_locations.details: Mainland bureaucracy and lodge where the affidavit route is found and Halima tells Nura about Saleha and Monir.

key_locations.name: Neighboring char tied to dredger work
key_locations.details: Tea stalls, dead pond, and pump yard where Nura traces Monir's dead-end paper trail.

key_locations.name: Mainland transfer landing
key_locations.details: Sorting point where verified slips split from appeal bundles and Halima gives Aminul the ledger.

key_locations.name: Nabinpara Relocation Colony
key_locations.details: Verified houses and edge barracks for appeals; Jamila C-12, Kashem Shed 4 Bay 7, Halima/Nura Shed 3 Bay 2.

distances: Halima's house is about a seven-minute walk from the school in dry weather and longer in mud.
The western edge sits far enough from school and mosque that the poorest widows can be missed unless summoned directly.
The ghat controls mainland travel and final evacuation order.
The neighboring char is close enough for a day trip but far enough to count as defiance.
Nabinpara is inland enough that road miles replace river time.

world_rules: Compensation and relocation placement are keyed to verified households, not mere physical presence.
Verification expects matching birth, marriage, land, and NID chains; old paper-only father lines now fail digital cross-checks.
The erosion household-affidavit route can save broken files, but only through public testimony, magistrate discretion, and contradictory witness scrutiny.
Char households often diverge from legal blood lines because of widow attachment, fosterage, remarriage, debt avoidance, and past paper interventions.
Documents are fragile: water, mold, rats, sweat, and handling can erase proof.
Boat schedules, office hours, launch departures, and travel costs are power.
Men speak more easily in public; women often hold the bodily and domestic facts that make households legible.
Witness time, photocopies, laminating, stamped paper, and transport all cost money.
The state keeps moving people even when appeals, grief, and private repair are unfinished.

power_structure.mechanism: Evacuation order, compensation rules, launch/truck allocation, and final household recognition.
Affidavit hearings, accepted/disputed rulings, and appeal status.

power_structure.over: How disputed cases are formatted, grouped, forwarded, and tied to evidence
power_structure.mechanism: Batch filing, office access, witness prep, and ledger chain of custody.

power_structure.over: Local rumor, queue order, witness pressure, and eastern-side paper legitimacy
power_structure.mechanism: Patronage, queue control, selective copied evidence, and public shaming.

power_structure.over: Hidden birth and household memory until the hearing
power_structure.mechanism: Midwife authority, memory, and the private ledger before chapter_18.
Control of documents, testimony, movement between roofs, and who gets spoken for.

objects.name: Oilcloth-wrapped birth ledger
objects.origin: Three decades of births and household adjustments: shifted dates, borrowed fathers, and hidden kin links.
objects.status: Hidden at story start, read at the chapter_15 hearing, then given to Aminul for appeals in chapter_18.

objects.name: Eastern-side copied ledger extracts
objects.origin: Selective pages Sufia copied the year after Saleha died from eastern high-ground entries.
objects.status: Used by Mofiz to stiffen claims; losses surface in chapter_08, and Sufia confirms the copies in chapter_14.

objects.name: Nura's certificate and clinic packet
objects.origin: SSC certificate, birth copy, school papers, and clinic forms built around Monir Hossain's paper father line.
objects.status: Kept in plastic or oilskin; usable for school proof but blocked by paternal mismatch and appeal status.

objects.name: Saleha's green shawl
objects.origin: Saleha's faded green shawl, kept on Halima's loft shelf after Saleha's death.
objects.status: Recovered in chapter_17, carried into relocation, and spread over the trunk overnight.

objects.name: Kashem's laminated identity card
objects.origin: National ID enrollment under the Abed Sheikh father line.
objects.status: Usable as identity proof, but not enough to reconcile his father line and household claim.

objects.name: Jamila's marriage and aunt-line household packet
objects.origin: Kabinnama, tax slips, death certificate, and related papers tied to the aunt-line Halima created to stop a debt marriage.
objects.status: Accepted under household affidavit in chapter_15 and carried into verified C-12 housing.

objects.name: Repayment booklet and engine-parts chit
objects.origin: Weekly poultry-collector marks plus the parts-seller note on Majed's engine debt.
objects.status: Used at the hearing to prove the Jamila-Majed household, then packed for relocation.

objects.name: Yellowed land-tax receipt bundles
objects.origin: Scattered proof of occupancy and small homestead claims kept in tins, trunks, and bedding.
objects.status: Water-damaged, rewrapped, and repeatedly used as thin documentary support.

debts.creditor: Local grocer supplying Rahima on credit
debts.deadline: Grocer note under Rahima's mat before her death
debts.status: Unpaid at Rahima's death; effectively inherited by Kashem.

debts.deadline: Before Majed can restart repair work after relocation
debts.status: Outstanding; used to prove the current household at the hearing.

debts.creditor: Poultry microcredit collector tied to Jamila's trade
debts.status: Current at the hearing but risky after relocation.

income_sources.source: Halima's midwifery payments in cash, rice, eggs, or favors
income_sources.reliability: Declining after the search and ledger scandal.
Moderate on the char; uncertain in Nabinpara.
Low and disrupted by appeal status and relocation.

income_sources.source: Jamila's dried fish, eggs, and poultry sales
income_sources.amount: Modest but steady when the household is stable
income_sources.reliability: Moderate, strengthened by verified C-12 placement.

income_sources.source: Majed's boat repair and ferry work
Mofiz's brokerage, fertilizer margin, and patronage cuts
income_sources.reliability: Strong while he keeps local leverage.

knowledge_state.knows: Kashem was born to Rahima and placed into the Sheikh household on night three so debt-linked men could not claim his labor.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: kashem_sheikh until chapter_15; officials until the hearing.

knowledge_state.knows: She is Kashem's biological mother and kept the secrecy that held him in the safer household line.
After Saleha died on the seventh day, Halima wrote 'For papers use Monir Hossain. Dredging line. No return to husband's people,' though she cannot swear he is Nura's biological father.
knowledge_state.learned_in: Before chapter_01; spoken aloud in chapter_10.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: nura_begum until chapter_10; officials until the hearing.

knowledge_state.knows: Saleha was her mother; Saleha died after a hard birth, and Halima used Monir Hossain as Nura's paper father to keep her at home and in school.
Monir's own papers carry conflicting father names and no stable NID or union trail, so he cannot repair her file.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: No one formally after chapter_12; office still sees only the paper father line.

knowledge_state.knows: The erosion household-affidavit route can move disputed files if a magistrate hears durable residence and dependency.
She copied selected eastern ledger pages the year after Saleha died and gave them to Mofiz as backup.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: halima_khatun until chapter_14; most of the char until the hearing.

knowledge_state.knows: The copied eastern pages can strengthen his claims and cast Halima's full ledger as self-serving forgery.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: District team and halima_khatun until chapter_14; most residents until the hearing.

knowledge_state.knows: Rahima is his mother; by chapter_16 he has called her Ma, though the file remains pending.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: No one after chapter_15; acceptance lags until Rahima's deathbed.

environmental_constants: Kadam Char smells of wet silt, fish oil, damp cloth, lamp smoke, and sour paper.
Tin roofs turn rain into noise and noon heat into pressure inside every room.
Mud slows walking, stains hems, swallows sandals, and damages documents and loads.
Paper curls, molds, blurs, and tears; people dry it under bowls, wrap it in plastic, and store it high.
The river is audible at night through engines, wake, and bank slippage, and it can take land without ceremony.
Private shame spreads fast because walls are thin and arguments, witness practice, and loading happen in public.
Nabinpara's dry dust and lime heat feel alien beside the char's damp air.
